bead.title: refactor(axon-schema): import named-query parser from axon-cypher-ast
bead.description:
After B1.1 (axon-1202db7c) extracts axon-cypher-ast as a leaf crate, switch axon-schema's named_queries.rs to depend on axon-cypher-ast directly instead of axon-cypher. This decouples axon-schema from the cypher executor, which is required so that axon-cypher can later depend on axon-storage without forming axon-storage → axon-schema → axon-cypher → axon-storage.

Today crates/axon-schema/src/named_queries.rs imports:
  - axon_cypher::ast::*
  - axon_cypher::schema::*
  - axon_cypher::{parse, plan, validate, CypherError}

After this bead, all of these come from axon_cypher_ast::* (same paths, exposed by the new leaf crate).

In-scope files:
  - crates/axon-schema/Cargo.toml (replace axon-cypher dep with axon-cypher-ast)
  - crates/axon-schema/src/named_queries.rs (rename `axon_cypher` → `axon_cypher_ast`)
  - crates/axon-schema/tests/* (only if any test imports axon_cypher; rename there too)

Out-of-scope:
  - axon-cypher's existing re-exports (kept intact for executor callers).
  - axon-storage move (handled in B1.3).
  - Any change to schema validation behavior or the named-queries surface.

Rollback: if a build error blocks completion, revert the dep swap and import rename atomically.
bead.acceptance:
AC1. crates/axon-schema/Cargo.toml has axon-cypher-ast (not axon-cypher) as a direct dep.
AC2. crates/axon-schema/src/named_queries.rs uses axon_cypher_ast:: paths only; no `axon_cypher::` references remain in axon-schema.
AC3. cargo tree -p axon-schema -e normal does not list axon-cypher as a direct dep (axon-cypher-ast is the only cypher-related entry).
AC4. cargo test -p axon-schema passes.
AC5. cargo test --workspace passes.
AC6. cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes.

events.body: {"rationale":"Well-formed refactor bead with clear motivation (cycle-breaking), precise scope, and verifiable ACs. Minor deductions: (1) AC3 conflates 'not a direct dep' with 'only cypher-related entry' — axon-cypher may still appear transitively and that is fine, so the AC wording could cause confusion; (2) description says 'same paths, exposed by the new leaf crate' but does not confirm axon-cypher-ast actually re-exports axon_cypher_ast::schema::* and the parse/plan/validate/CypherError surface — if the new crate has different module layout the AC2 rename is incomplete; (3) no mention of updating root Cargo.toml workspace.dependencies if axon-cypher-ast needs to be added there; (4) rollback instruction is good but could name the exact commit or tag to roll back to for clarity.","score":92,"suggested_fixes":["AC3: Clarify wording to 'axon-cypher does not appear as a **direct** dependency of axon-schema in cargo tree output' to avoid false failures from transitive appearances.","Description: Confirm (or add AC) that axon-cypher-ast exposes all symbols currently imported from axon-cypher (ast::*, schema::*, parse, plan, validate, CypherError) so implementors do not have to guess at the new crate's surface.","Add an implicit AC or in-scope file: if axon-cypher-ast is not yet in [workspace.dependencies] in the root Cargo.toml, that file is also in scope for the dep swap.","Rollback: reference the parent commit hash (axon-1202db7c merge commit) as the known-good point rather than 'atomically revert' to make rollback unambiguous."],"waivers_applied":[]}
